name: Frontend Bundle Size Optimization
description: Reduce JavaScript bundle size for faster page loads through tree-shaking, code splitting, and dependency optimization.

constraints: All existing UI features must remain functional
No visual regressions in core components
Do not remove any user-facing functionality

primary.command: npm run build 2>&1 && cat dist/stats.json | node -e "const d=require('fs').readFileSync('/dev/stdin','utf8');const s=JSON.parse(d);console.log('bundle_kb:',Math.round(s.totalSize/1024))"

secondary.name: chunk_count
secondary.command: ls dist/assets/*.js 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ' | xargs -I{} echo 'chunks: {}'

agent_instructions: You are reducing the JavaScript bundle size. Focus on: (1) replacing heavy libraries with lighter alternatives (e.g., date-fns instead of moment), (2) dynamic imports for lazy-loaded routes, (3) tree-shaking unused exports by reviewing named vs default imports, (4) removing unused dependencies from package.json, (5) configuring Vite/Rollup manual chunks to split the bundle into more parallel-loadable chunks (more chunks = better, measured by chunk_count). Make one change per trial.
